title: Why This Requires a Shared Understanding
concept: For a prompt like 'a red bicycle' to produce the right image, the model needs a shared notion of what 'red' and 'bicycle' mean across both text and vision.

exercises.prompt: If you tell a friend 'draw a red bicycle,' what do you both need to already agree on?

options.text: What the weather is like today

exercises.explain: To draw the right picture, you and your friend both need to share the same idea of 'red' and 'bicycle.' AI needs that shared understanding too!

cards.title: Same Words, Same Picture?
cards.body: You type 'a red bicycle' and an AI draws it. But how does the AI know that 'red' means that color and 'bicycle' means that object? It needs to connect words to pictures in a way that matches how we understand them!

cards.title: Shared Understanding
cards.body: Shared understanding means the AI's 'idea' of a word (like 'red') and its 'idea' of the matching picture (a red color) line up. Text and images are linked to the same meaning, so the model knows what to draw when you type words.

cards.title: Two Translators, One Language
cards.body: Imagine two translators who never talked to each other before. If one thinks 'red' means 'angry' and the other thinks it means the color, they'll mess up! AI needs its 'word brain' and 'picture brain' to agree on meanings, just like translators need to agree on a language.

cards.title: Red Bicycle, Not Blue Car
cards.body: When you type 'a red bicycle,' the AI's text understanding lights up the ideas of 'red' (color) and 'bicycle' (object with wheels). Its image-making part uses those SAME ideas to paint a red-colored bike, not a blue car or a red apple.

cards.title: Myth: Words and Pictures Are Separate
cards.body: Myth: The AI just guesses randomly what words might look like. Truth: The AI is trained on tons of paired examples (like a photo of a red bike labeled 'red bicycle') so it learns a shared meaning connecting words and images.

cards.title: Matching Meanings
cards.body: When word-meaning and picture-meaning are linked, the AI creates exactly what you asked for. When they aren't linked well, you get weird, wrong images.

left.text: 'Red bicycle' → AI draws a red bike, because 'red' and 'bicycle' mean the same thing in text and image

right.text: 'Red bicycle' → AI draws a blue skateboard, because words and pictures aren't connected properly

exercises.prompt: Why does an AI need a shared understanding of 'red' and 'bicycle'?

options.text: So it can pick random colors and shapes
So the words and the image mean the same thing to the AI
So it can skip drawing the bicycle

exercises.explain: The AI needs its text meaning and image meaning to match, so typing 'red bicycle' actually produces a picture of a red bicycle.

exercises.prompt: True or False: An AI can draw 'a red bicycle' correctly even if it has never connected the word 'red' to the color red in images.
exercises.explain: False! Without linking the word 'red' to the actual color in images, the AI wouldn't know what color to paint the bicycle.

exercises.sentence: For AI to draw 'a red bicycle' correctly, it needs a ___ understanding of what 'red' and 'bicycle' mean in both words and pictures.
exercises.explain: 'Shared' understanding means the text meaning and image meaning of 'red' and 'bicycle' match up for the AI.

exercises.prompt: Which AI response shows a GOOD shared understanding of the prompt 'a red bicycle'?

options.text: A picture of a shiny red bicycle with two wheels
A picture of a green bus

exercises.explain: A red bicycle picture shows the AI correctly matched both the color 'red' and the object 'bicycle' between text and image.

exercises.prompt: You ask an AI for 'a red bicycle' and it draws a picture of a yellow banana instead. What most likely went wrong?

options.text: The AI's text meaning and image meaning of 'red' and 'bicycle' were not properly linked
The AI ran out of red paint
Bicycles are too hard to draw
The AI understood perfectly and this was correct

exercises.explain: When the shared understanding between words and images breaks down, the AI can't correctly turn your words into the right picture, leading to a totally wrong result like a banana instead of a bicycle!
